page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 354.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 1.97 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

The AMD EPYC processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as high-throughput memory bandwidth, reaching 346.92 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads.

The instance excels in parallel processing tasks, showing top-tier results in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and database operations. It also achieves high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. For machine learning, the processor's vector extensions enable top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium LLM models. However, with only 1.97 GB of RAM per vCPU and no local storage, it presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications or workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highcpu-180 is structured to maximize compute density. By minimizing the RAM-to-vCPU ratio, it avoids the premium costs associated with large memory allocations, making it highly efficient for compute-heavy, CPU-bound tasks. Recommended workloads include large-scale software compilation, high-concurrency web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, parallel simulations, and compute-intensive database operations.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers 180 dedicated vCPUs across 90 physical cores, paired with 354.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a lean 1.97 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, prioritizing pure CPU compute density. It features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations. It also performs exceptionally well in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks by avoiding excess memory overhead. It is highly suited for parallel computing, software build pipelines, high-throughput web serving, and compute-heavy database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server featuring 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 354 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server with 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 354 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring 180 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs for parallel processing and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 180 dedicated vCPUs and 90 physical cores.
Features 354 GB of RAM, optimized for high compute-to-memory efficiency.
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration instructions.
Delivers top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and database throughput.
Best suited for CPU-bound parallel processing, web serving, and machine learning inference.
